name: Carolina Specialty Transport
districts_or_customers: Metrolina Region of NC & SC (Gaston County area); reportedly contracted by local schools to transport special-needs children; serves Medicaid/NEMT clientele
recent_news: Long-established (since 1996); no recent M&A news identified. Some BBB complaints around service coordination but no ownership change signals.
fit_notes: Small family-owned NC special-needs/NEMT operator with ~35 vehicles in Summit's Carolinas footprint; primarily NEMT/special-needs rather than yellow-bus, so fit as a tuck-in is moderate (could add SPED capability in Charlotte metro). Founder-led; possible succession candidate given 30-year operating history.

name: Wheelcare Express Inc
districts_or_customers: Northern California: North Bay (Marin, Santa Rosa, Mendocino, Lake, Napa, Solano), East Bay (Alameda, Contra Costa, parts of Santa Clara), San Francisco/Peninsula (SF, San Mateo). Primarily medical/dialysis/oncology/rehab clients; no confirmed school district contracts.
recent_news: 40+ years in business per company site; no M&A or ownership change news identified.
fit_notes: Pure-play NEMT/paratransit operator with ~100-vehicle fleet across Northern California; no evidence of school bus or student transportation contracts. Limited tuck-in fit for Summit School Services' yellow-bus/SPED platform; would be off-thesis unless Summit pursues NEMT/SPED diversification in CA.

name: Accessible Vans Of NC
owner_or_parent: Ilderton Conversion Company (family-owned wheelchair van dealer/converter, est. 1972)
districts_or_customers: Sells/converts mobility vans for personal, commercial, school, and paratransit end-users; not a transportation operator. 701 S Main St, High Point matches Ilderton Conversions High Point footprint (BraunAbility certified dealer).
recent_news: Ilderton Conversion has operated since 1972; family-owned dealer/converter with locations in High Point, Charlotte, Charleston, Asheville, Myrtle Beach. No M&A news.
fit_notes: NOT A TUCK-IN TARGET: This is a wheelchair van dealer/converter (Ilderton Conversion Co.), not a student-transportation operator. They sell/build vehicles that operators (potentially including Summit) buy. Exclude from acquisition pipeline; could be a vendor relationship.

name: Lakes Medi-Van
districts_or_customers: 200-mile radius of Detroit Lakes including Bemidji, Brainerd, Worthington, Windom, Cambridge; Minnesota Medicaid NEMT; serves people with intellectual disabilities/specialized needs; no confirmed K-12 school district contracts.
recent_news: 41 years in business as of 2026 (founded Feb 1, 1985); BBB-accredited since 2008. Blue Cross MN reasserted control over rural Medicaid rides — modest industry headwind.
fit_notes: Founder-led 40+ year MN NEMT/special-needs operator with strong rural footprint; principal husband-wife team suggests possible succession opportunity. Off-strategy for yellow-bus thesis but could fit a SPED/NEMT adjacency play in MN. Modest scale; rural Medicaid rate headwinds.

name: Royal Xpress Inc
districts_or_customers: Illinois (Lake County/Zion-Wadsworth area and 'beyond'); offers school and employee bus transportation plus special-needs/NEMT; specific district names not publicly disclosed.
recent_news: Family-owned small operator since 2009; no M&A or material news identified.
fit_notes: Small (~15 employee) family-owned IL operator that advertises school bus + special-needs + employee shuttle services; sub-scale for a meaningful tuck-in on its own, but could fit a Lake County/north-suburban Chicago route-density play if Summit is building IL presence. Low public visibility — would need direct outreach to verify fleet/district contracts.
